inline_comments.message: it looks to me like could use generic methods and parameters to collapse and/or refactor most of these methods - probably only need one flavor of each (may still need all three flavors of these top-level methods, but they can probably be shortened to one-liners invoking a common, generic method)
Sounds like a good idea but I don't fully get it. Do you mean factory pattern? It would be appreciated if you can expand a little bit more.

of course, this test could also be deleted:

    if (!policyIdentifiers.isEmpty())

In theory, only one policy with a policyId should be deployed.   This will have to change once that is supported in the PAP.   Question:  I assume so, but do regular tosca policies (non-legacy) also return a list for the "deployed" API call.   Just asking to make sure the interface is consistent for both for CLAMP to use (they want to test with this fix).
We purposely modified PAP to only allow one version of a policy to be deployed.
Oh, so this already went in in El Alto, you're confirming right?   I have some very vague recollection, but wasn't sure.
Only one version of a policy to be deployed in each PDP instance or PDP subgroup or PDP group? which granularity?
